title: mona meridensis
side_effects: Mona meridensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to these toxins can also lead to liver and kidney damage, as well as gastrointestinal problems like diarrhea and stomach pain. In severe cases, improper use of the Mona meridensis plant can cause respiratory failure, seizures, and even coma. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using the plant for medicinal purposes to avoid these potential side effects.
